rag.description: Retrieval Augmented Generation - traditional and advanced

questions.question: What's your biggest RAG failure in production?
questions.context: I asked 50 engineers building RAG systems in production
questions.follow_up: Drop your story in comments. What went wrong and how did you fix it?

questions.question: Where are you in your RAG journey?
questions.context: I've worked with teams at different stages. Here's the pattern I see:
options: Stage 3: Multiple systems, hunting for patterns
Stage 5: Moving beyond RAG to Agentic
questions.follow_up: What's your stage? And what's the biggest challenge at YOUR stage?

questions.question: Should we RAG this or build it traditional?
questions.context: I see teams debating this constantly. Here's when each makes sense:

Traditional (No RAG).cons: Limited to training data, outdated knowledge

questions.follow_up: What did you choose? And would you pick the same today?

questions.question: How do you chunk your documents for RAG?
questions.context: This might seem simple but it's where most RAG systems fail
questions.follow_up: What's your strategy? And did you try others first?

questions.question: One RAG mistake that changed how I build systems
questions.context: I used to think better documents = better RAG. I was wrong.
questions.story_arc: The mistake → Why it happened → What I learned → How I changed
questions.follow_up: What's the RAG mistake that taught you the most?

questions.question: I asked 30 engineers: Should you build Agentic AI NOW?
questions.context: The debate: Is Agentic AI hype or essential? Here's what they said:
poll_options: YES - It's the future, start now
NO - Still too complex and risky
WAIT - Let it mature another 6 months
questions.follow_up: Where do you stand? And what's holding you back or pushing you forward?

questions.question: Agentic AI: Is the complexity worth it?
questions.context: Going Agentic adds: 3x complexity, latency, harder debugging... but smarter decisions
questions.follow_up: If you went Agentic, was it worth it? Would you do it again?

questions.question: What's your biggest Agentic AI challenge?
questions.context: Not everyone's building Agentic yet, but those who are face hard problems
challenges: Tool selection - Which tools should agents have access to?
Looping - Agents get stuck in infinite loops
Debugging - When it fails, why? Hard to tell
Latency - Too slow for real-time use cases
questions.follow_up: Which resonates? And how did you solve it (or are you still solving)?

questions.question: My Agentic AI disaster (and what I learned)
questions.context: I built an agent that looked perfect in testing. Production told a different story.
questions.story_arc: What I built → What went wrong → Why it surprised me → How I fixed it
questions.follow_up: Did you have an Agentic failure? Share it. We all learn from disasters.

questions.question: How many tools should your agent have?
questions.context: This is the paradox: More tools = more possibilities, but also more failures
perspectives: Hierarchical - Agents specialized by domain (best of both)
questions.follow_up: How many tools does your agent have? Would you increase or decrease?

llm_optimization.description: Making LLMs faster, cheaper, and more reliable

questions.question: When optimizing LLMs, what matters most to you?
questions.context: Speed, accuracy, cost, reliability - pick your battles
options: SPEED - Users won't wait for slow responses
COST - Margins are tight, every token counts
ACCURACY - A wrong answer is worse than slow
TRADEOFF - It's all balanced, context matters
questions.follow_up: What's your top priority? And has it changed over time?

questions.question: Is fine-tuning worth it? Or just prompt optimization?
questions.context: Debate: Spend 1 week on perfect prompts vs 1 month on fine-tuning
perspectives: PROMPTS ONLY - 80% of the value, 5% of the cost
fine-tuning WORTH IT - Better control, consistent
HYBRID - Start with prompts, fine-tune if needed
DEPENDS - Your data quality determines viability
questions.follow_up: Which path did you take? Happy with it or regret it?

questions.question: Tell me about your LLM cost explosion
questions.context: I know I'm not alone. Your LLM bill surprised you. Here's mine...
questions.story_arc: What you built → Cost looked fine → Scaling happened → Bill shocked you
questions.follow_up: How did you fix it? And what would you do differently?

questions.question: How do you prevent LLM hallucinations?
questions.context: Hallucinations cost me months. Here's what finally worked...
questions.follow_up: What works for you? And what doesn't?

questions.question: Monolith or Microservices for your AI system?
questions.context: The classic debate hits different when LLMs are involved
options: HYBRID - Monolith for LLM, services for supporting
questions.follow_up: What architecture do you regret? What would you choose today?

questions.question: What's your AI system's biggest bottleneck?
questions.context: I see patterns across production systems. Most bottlenecks aren't where you'd think
bottlenecks: LLM latency - Model inference too slow
questions.follow_up: Where's your bottle neck? And how did you discover it?

questions.question: How I scaled my AI system from 10 to 10,000 users
questions.context: Nothing breaks assumptions like 100x growth. Here's what broke for me...
questions.story_arc: What worked for 10 → What failed at 100 → What failed at 1000 → What finally scaled
questions.follow_up: What scaling lesson cost you the most (in time, money, or credibility)?

questions.question: Reliability vs Innovation: Which wins in your system?
questions.context: Fast iteration breaks things. Stability slows you down. Where's your balance?

tradeoffs.Stability First: 99.9% uptime, slow to iterate, user trust high
tradeoffs.Innovation First: Frequent changes, some breakage, faster learning
tradeoffs.Balanced: Staged rollout, good metrics, middle ground

questions.follow_up: Where are you on this spectrum? Happy with it?

tools_frameworks.description: LlamaIndex vs LangChain, Claude vs GPT-4, etc.

questions.question: LangChain vs LlamaIndex: Which one and why?
questions.context: I asked 40 engineers which they'd choose today. The answers surprised me
questions.follow_up: Which did you pick? Do you regret it or double down?

questions.question: Claude vs GPT-4: Which one powers your system?
questions.context: The LLM choice shapes everything. Here's what I see across teams
comparison_points: Quality - Different strengths on different tasks
Use Case - Matters more than model
questions.follow_up: Which do you use? And would you switch?

questions.question: The framework choice I regret most
questions.context: Picking the right tool matters. Picking wrong costs months.
questions.story_arc: What I chose → Why it made sense then → Why it was wrong → What I learned
questions.follow_up: What tool/framework choice haunts you? Would you switch?

questions.question: One skill that made me a better AI engineer
questions.context: People focus on frameworks. But there's ONE skill that changed everything for me
questions.follow_up: What ONE skill would you learn if you could?

questions.question: The biggest mistake I made as an AI engineer (and how I fixed it)
questions.context: Early on, I thought X. I was so wrong. Here's what changed my perspective
questions.story_arc: What I believed → Why it was wrong → What I learned → How I changed
questions.follow_up: What was YOUR biggest professional lesson in AI?

questions.question: Still feel like an imposter? You're not alone.
questions.context: I build LLM systems professionally. I still don't feel like an expert.
themes: Field moves too fast to master
So many unknowns and edge cases
Learn by shipping, not by reading
questions.follow_up: Do you feel it too? What helps you push through?

questions.question: Hot take: Is RAG dying?
questions.context: Everyone pivots to Agentic. But is RAG actually dead or just misapplied?
perspectives: RAG is DEAD - Agentic is the future
RAG is ESSENTIAL - Still core of many systems
RAG is EVOLVING - Becoming foundation for Agentic
Context-dependent - Different use cases, different answers
questions.follow_up: What do you think? Is your next project RAG or Agentic?

questions.question: Will we see another AI winter?
questions.context: History rhymes. AI bubbles have burst before. Will it happen again?
options: YES - Hype will fade, most startups will fail
NO - LLMs are fundamental, here to stay
questions.follow_up: What's your prediction? And how would that change your choices?

questions.question: What scares you about AI replacing engineers?
questions.context: It's okay to be worried. Everyone should think about this.
questions.follow_up: What actually scares you? And how are you preparing?

structure: HOOK - I asked X engineers one question
PERSONAL_TAKE - But here's what I think they missed
CTA - Drop YOUR answer in comments
SETUP - The debate I see constantly
BOTH_SIDES - Here's the case for each
MY_TAKE - This is what I'd pick (and why)
CONTEXT - Here's where I see this problem
MY_APPROACH - This is what works for me

posting_rules.cycle_through_topics: RAG → Agentic → LLM Opt → System Design → Tools → Career → Trends
posting_rules.engagement_check: Respond to top 10 comments within 4 hours
posting_rules.follow_on_posts: If question gets 200+ comments, write follow-up post with insights
